title: Runebound Retrospective - Episode 7 - Runebound 3rd edition
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rqcOPT_Dg7A

The retrospective series reaches its conclusion with Daniel (@dungeondive) examining Runebound's 2015 redesign for third edition. This ambitious reimagining shifts from the core mechanics that defined second edition, introducing new hero progression systems and overhauled challenge structures. While third edition brings fresh approaches to character customization and advancement, it's also a game that requires careful rules reading and table adjudication.

The episode explores third edition's strengths and limitations honestly, analyzing how it compares to its predecessor and considering what worked (and didn't) in Fantasy Flight's modernization attempt. With fewer expansions than second edition ever received, third edition remains something of a curiosity in the Runebound lineage—a worthy experimental entry that found its own dedicated following despite the overwhelming fan preference for second edition's modular, hackable design.

----

Have you played both editions of Runebound? Which version's approach to progression and character development resonates more with your playstyle?
